title: URIs, URL Resolution, Origins, DNS, and Destination Identity · Browser and Node Client Systems · Inference Brief: Destination Validation
summary: A Node-based partner SDK accepts tenant endpoints and must preserve URL, origin, IDN, DNS, and certificate distinctions across environments. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts destination validation increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch destination-resolution and origin contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Canonicalize and validate schemes, hosts, IP literals, ports, IDNs, and resolved destinations before granting network authority. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Trace a hostname through DNS answers, address selection, route, port, connection, and certificate identity. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
